{{Expand Japanese|畝尾都多本神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Uneotsutamoto Shrine | native_name = 畝尾都多本神社 | image = 畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Uneotsutamoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612}} }} {{nihongo|'''Uneotsutamoto Shrine'''|畝尾都多本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Q135160338|lt=Q135160338|WD=Q135160338}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135009152|lt=Q135009152|WD=Q135009152}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|lt=Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} ** Series ordinal: 199 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039032|lt=Q135039032|WD=Q135039032}} ** P156: {{ill|Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|lt=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|WD=Q135039033}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q496319|lt=Q496319|WD=Q496319}} * {{ill|Q907495|lt=Q907495|WD=Q907495}} * {{ill|Toichi district|lt=Toichi district|ja|十市郡|zh|十市郡|WD=Q11405036}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} == image (P18) == * 畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180858 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|lt=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * うねおつたもとじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005004870 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本144', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:畝尾都多本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28685121}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uneotsutamoto Shrine |画像 = [[Image:畝尾都多本神社 - Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}144 Kinomoto | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 54 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} |別名 = Nakisawa Shrine |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Uneotsutamoto Shrine''' (うねおつたもとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおつたもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{Expand Japanese|畝尾都多本神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Uneotsutamoto Shrine | native_name = 畝尾都多本神社 | image = 畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Uneotsutamoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612}} }} {{nihongo|'''Uneotsutamoto Shrine'''|畝尾都多本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Q135160338|lt=Q135160338|WD=Q135160338}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135009152|lt=Q135009152|WD=Q135009152}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|lt=Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} ** Series ordinal: 199 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039032|lt=Q135039032|WD=Q135039032}} ** P156: {{ill|Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|lt=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|WD=Q135039033}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q496319|lt=Q496319|WD=Q496319}} * {{ill|Q907495|lt=Q907495|WD=Q907495}} * {{ill|Toichi district|lt=Toichi district|ja|十市郡|zh|十市郡|WD=Q11405036}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} == image (P18) == * 畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180858 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|lt=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * うねおつたもとじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005004870 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本144', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:畝尾都多本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28685121}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uneotsutamoto Shrine |画像 = [[Image:畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}144 Kinomoto | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 54 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} |別名 = Nakisawa Shrine |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Uneotsutamoto Shrine''' (うねおつたもとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおつたもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{Expand Japanese|畝尾都多本神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Uneotsutamoto Shrine | native_name = 畝尾都多本神社 | image = 畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Uneotsutamoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612|1=Nakisawame}} }} {{nihongo|'''Uneotsutamoto Shrine'''|畝尾都多本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|WD=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135009152|ltq=Q135009152|WD=Q135009152|1=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|ltq=Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 199 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039032|ltq=Q135039032|WD=Q135039032|1=Koheno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|lt=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|WD=Q135039033|1=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q496319|ltq=Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} * {{ill|Q907495|ltq=Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Toichi district|lt=Toichi district|ja|十市郡|zh|十市郡|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} == image (P18) == * 畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612|1=Nakisawame}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180858 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * うねおつたもとじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005004870 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本144', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:畝尾都多本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28685121}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uneotsutamoto Shrine |画像 = [[Image:畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}144 Kinomoto | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 54 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |別名 = Nakisawa Shrine |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Uneotsutamoto Shrine''' (うねおつたもとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187|1=Ruijū Karin}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401|1=Hinokuma no Ōkimi}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)|1=696}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590|1=Prince Takechi}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835|1=Kojiki-den}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591|1=shōzei}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおつたもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおつたもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|WD=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135009152|ltq=Q135009152|WD=Q135009152|1=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|ltq=Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 199 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039032|ltq=Q135039032|WD=Q135039032|1=Koheno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|lt=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|WD=Q135039033|1=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q496319|ltq=Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} * {{ill|Q907495|ltq=Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Toichi district|lt=Toichi district|ja|十市郡|zh|十市郡|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} == image (P18) == * 畝尾都多本神社 - Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}144 Kinomoto | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 54 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |別名 = Nakisawa Shrine |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Uneotsutamoto Shrine''' (うねおつたもとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187|1=Ruijū Karin}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401|1=Hinokuma no Ōkimi}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)|1=696}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590|1=Prince Takechi}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835|1=Kojiki-den}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591|1=shōzei}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおつたもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835|1=Kojiki-den}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591|1=shōzei}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うねおつたもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|WD=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135009152|ltq=Q135009152|WD=Q135009152|1=Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|ltq=Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 199 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039032|ltq=Q135039032|WD=Q135039032|1=Koheno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|lt=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine|WD=Q135039033|1=Amanokakoyamano- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q496319|ltq=Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} * {{ill|Q907495|ltq=Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Toichi district|lt=Toichi district|ja|十市郡|zh|十市郡|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.498687|135.815007}} == image (P18) == * 畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612|1=Nakisawame}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180858 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * うねおつたもとじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005004870 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本144', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:畝尾都多本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28685121}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uneotsutamoto Shrine |画像 = [[Image:畝尾都多本神社 - {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}144 Kinomoto | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 54 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |別名 = Nakisawa Shrine |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Uneotsutamoto Shrine''' (うねおつたもとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187|1=Ruijū Karin}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401|1=Hinokuma no Ōkimi}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)|1=696}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590|1=Prince Takechi}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835|1=Kojiki-den}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591|1=shōzei}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187|1=Ruijū Karin}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401|1=Hinokuma no Ōkimi}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)|1=696}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590|1=Prince Takechi}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835|1=Kojiki-den}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591|1=shōzei}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}144 Kinomoto | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 54 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |別名 = Nakisawa Shrine |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Uneotsutamoto Shrine''' (うねおつたもとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187|1=Ruijū Karin}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401|1=Hinokuma no Ōkimi}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)|1=696}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590|1=Prince Takechi}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835|1=Kojiki-den}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591|1=shōzei}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Nakisawame|lt=Nakisawame|en|Nakisawame|ja|ナキサワメ|zh|泣澤女神|WD=Q11144612|1=Nakisawame}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180858 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * うねおつたもとじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005004870 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本144', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木之本町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:畝尾都多本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28685121}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uneotsutamoto Shrine |画像 = [[Image:畝尾都多本神社 - DGX b6pV0AAhKk2.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}144 Kinomoto | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 54 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |別名 = Nakisawa Shrine |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Uneotsutamoto Shrine''' (うねおつたもとじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is located on the northwestern foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Amanokagu|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}}, and to the adjacent northeast stands another Shikinaisha, {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. It is also called "Nakisawa Shrine" (written with characters such as 啼澤, 哭沢, 泣沢) and is known as "Nakisawa no Mori." {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYD2GRV0AIIJd6.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Haiden]]}} {{右|[[画像:畝尾都多本神社 - DGYEGSoVwAE5PVn.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Sacred well (shintai)]]}} === Enshrined deity === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なきさわめのかみ) There is a well called Nakisawa, and that well is worshiped as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō. === History === The founding is unknown, but in Book 2, poem 202 of the '{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}': :Though sacred sake is offered and prayers made at Nakisawa Shrine, my jewel child does not know the high sun. :(Even though sacred sake is offered to the goddess of Nakisawa Shrine and prayers are made to prolong the life of the deceased prince, the prince has finally gone to rule the heavens.) The left-side note records: :"This one poem, according to {{ill|lt=Ruijū Karin|Q11665187|WD=Q11665187|1=Ruijū Karin}}, is said to be a song by {{ill|lt=Hinokuma no Ōkimi|Q30932401|WD=Q30932401|1=Hinokuma no Ōkimi}} lamenting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakisawa-me-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}. Looking at the Nihongi: in autumn, seventh month, on the day Kōjutsu, in the 10th year (696), later prince no mikoto passed away." This is a waka of resentment toward Nakisawa-me-no-Kami: in {{ill|lt=696|Q23486|WD=Q23486|lt=Jitō Tennō 10 (696)|1=696}}, Matsukuma no Ōkimi offered sacred sake to a revival deity and prayed for {{ill|lt=Prince Takechi|Q837590|WD=Q837590|1=Prince Takechi}}'s life, but he did not return to life{{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. From this, Uneotsutamoto Shrine is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Asuka period|Q210688|WD=Q210688|1=Asuka period}}. On a precinct stone monument, in addition to the above Man'yōshū annotation, are engraved a passage from {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|WD=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}}'s 'Tamasuki' saying "Nakisawa-me deity is a deity for life-begging," and a quotation from {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}}'s '{{ill|lt=Kojikiden|Q11410835|WD=Q11410835|1=Kojiki-den}}' saying "etymologically, sawa-me connects to rain and is a water deity"[http://engishiki.org/yamato/html/031410-01.html Uneotsutamoto Shrine - Investigation of Engishiki shrines]. In the 1446 (Bun'an 3) 'Washū Gokōri Jinja Jinmyōchō Tairyaku Chūkai,' together with {{ill|lt=Amanokagu-yama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|lt=Amanokagu ni Imasu Kushimakoto Shrine|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, Sakamon Shrine (Ame-no-Iwato Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}, it is referred to as "Amanokagu-yama ni Imasu Yotsudokoro Shrines"[https://genbu.net/data/yamato/tutamoto_title.htm Uneotsutamoto Shrine]. It is identified with Uneotsutamoto Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and stands adjacent to the also-listed {{ill|lt=Uneo ni Imasu Taketsuchi-yasu Shrine|Q30931377|WD=Q30931377|1=Uneoza Taketsuyasugami Shrine}}. Remains of the "Kaguyama Shōsō," an office that stored and managed {{ill|lt=shōzei|Q11545591|WD=Q11545591|1=shōzei}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, have been excavated nearby. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

The shrine buildings were rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era. === Name origin === "Une" means a wavy/ridged place. "O" means where a mountain foot stretches long like an animal tail. "Uneo" thus means mountain foot; this refers to the foot of Mount Kagu. === Sessha/Massha === *Hachiman Shrine (deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}} · {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} · {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime Ōkami|1=Empress Jingū}}) *Inari Shrine (deity - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) === References === *{{Cite book |和書 |author=Masashi Kinoshita (ed.) |year=2016 |title=Asuka Historic Sites Encyclopedia |page=201-205 |publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}} |location= |isbn=978-4-642-08290-7 |quote= }} ===Notes=== {{Reflist}} === External link === *[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/uneotsutamoto.html Kashihara City / Uneotsutamoto Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uneotsutamoto Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]
